text: Build an emergency fund covering 3-6 months of expenses before investing; it keeps a setback from becoming debt.
Pay credit-card balances in full each month - carrying a balance means paying interest that often exceeds 20% a year.
Check your credit report yearly for free at annualcreditreport.com to catch errors and signs of identity theft early.
A smoke alarm halves the risk of dying in a home fire; test it monthly and replace batteries yearly.
For a kitchen grease fire, turn off the heat and cover the pan with a lid - never use water, which spreads burning oil.
Know how to shut off your home's main water valve; acting fast during a burst pipe can prevent major damage.
The 50/30/20 budget is a simple start: 50% needs, 30% wants, 20% savings and debt payoff.
Hand-washing with soap for 20 seconds is one of the most effective ways to prevent the spread of illness.
Keep a basic emergency kit: water, non-perishable food, flashlight, first-aid supplies, and copies of key documents.
Reading a product's unit price (price per ounce or liter), not the sticker price, reveals the real better deal.
Compound interest works for savers and against borrowers: starting to save early beats saving more later.
Learn the Heimlich maneuver and hands-only CPR; both are simple to learn and can save a life before help arrives.
